methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Naifo Technologies is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Gaëtan Sévérac, having raised €54.7M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, comprising eight private firms and one public comparable (277810.KQ). Key private peers include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

A peer-median approach was applied, reflecting standard valuation practice for frontier-tech companies at this stage where transaction data remains limited. The nine-company peer cohort yielded a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business models, capital efficiency, and perceived market opportunity within the robotics sector.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B (low) to $1.8B (high), centered on $1.6B. This range anchors to peer-median methodology given the absence of other applicable approaches (revenue multiples or DCF analysis are not supported by the data provided). Key uncertainties include differentiation relative to the peer set, capital deployment efficiency versus raised capital, and market validation milestones achieved post-Series B.
